title: Caring for a Bleeding or Cracked Elbow Callus on Your Dog
meta_desc: Is your dog’s elbow callus cracking or bleeding? Learn what causes pressure sores, the urgency level, and how to triage your pet's skin health.
content_html:
What is an Elbow Callus?

An elbow callus, also known as a pressure sore or hyperkeratosis, is a thick, hairless patch of skin that forms over the bony joints of a dog. These are most common in large or heavy breeds. The skin thickens as a protective measure against the constant friction and impact of lying on hard surfaces. Over time, these patches can become excessively dry, leading to painful cracking and minor bleeding.

Is This an Emergency?

The urgency level for a cracked or bleeding callus is typically Low. While it is uncomfortable and can lead to infection if left untreated, it is rarely a life-threatening emergency. You should contact your veterinarian during regular business hours. However, if you notice signs of a deep infection, such as foul odors, significant swelling, heat, or yellow discharge (pus), you should seek veterinary attention more promptly.

How Photos Help Triage

When you provide a clear, well-lit photograph of the affected elbow, it allows a veterinary professional to assess the severity of the tissue damage. A photo helps us determine if the issue is a simple surface crack or if it has progressed into a hygroma (a fluid-filled sac) or a deep-seated bacterial infection. Seeing the color and depth of the wound helps us decide whether you need an immediate prescription or if simple environmental changes can manage the condition.

faqs.question: Can I use human moisturizer on my dog's callus?
faqs.answer: It is best to avoid human lotions, as many contain fragrances or chemicals like zinc oxide that can be toxic if your dog licks them. Use only veterinarian-recommended, pet-safe balms.

faqs.question: How can I stop the callus from bleeding?
faqs.answer: Apply gentle pressure with a clean cloth to stop any active bleeding. Keeping your dog on soft surfaces like rugs or orthopedic beds will help prevent further trauma to the area.

faqs.question: Will the hair ever grow back on the callus?
faqs.answer: In many cases, once a callus has formed, the hair follicles are permanently damaged. However, keeping the skin supple can prevent the callus from getting larger or more irritated.
